core_loop: Each season a faction spends Domain Action slots across 7 turn priorities (Domain: govern/muster/conquest/transfer; Social: Parliament/Treaty; Special: 1 signature action/faction), then 10-step Accounting applies stat deltas, Stability/collapse checks, CI/RS/IP clocks, Turmoil/Accord, and victory check (GD-1: 11+ territories, 2 seasons). Underneath, a 4-component engine (Mission/Cascade/Public Expectation/Legitimacy+PS) computes disposition via named-coefficient formulas each season, feeding Mandate — the headline stat gating each faction's one signature action.

decision_points.options: a handful of named verbs, each a single roll vs Ob with 2-4 rung degree ladder
10 fixed motions, Mandate-weighted votes, Church veto cadence, Rebuttal math
none directly — formula-computed via α/β/γ/λ coefficients + cosine cascade fidelity
literally GM-adjudicated; Guilds reduced to 3 hardcoded if/then rules
all 6 factions default single-root, deferred to Stage 10

north_star.collision_feed: Feeds the collision engine mainly via Standing/CB/Conviction-Scar accumulation, Mandate-gated signature actions, and Foil/Axis positioning (good seeding for cross-faction collisions like Excommunication chains, Consecration Triangle) — but a large share of 'faction behavior' is accounting output, not player choice.
north_star.verdict: Points at the North Star narratively (rich faction sheets/arcs/foils) but the mechanical decision surface it actually offers per season is narrow, and its autonomous-world claim is undermined by an explicit GM-controlled NPC-vote instruction.

nq.observation: faction_layer_v30 §5.8 states 'GM controls NPC faction votes (Guilds, Niflhel...)' — directly conflicts with the no-GM engine premise (Omega clause c, autonomous world) and CLAUDE.md's 'there is no GM' framing.

Dramatic-legibility partial: Mission/Doctrine Notes answer 'what does each actor want' cleanly, but 'whose position is at risk' requires cross-referencing 5 Stability Triggers with no single surfaced threshold line.

The 4-component behavioral engine (Mission/Cascade/Public Expectation/L+PS, with temperament typology and cosine-similarity fidelity) is heavy machinery whose sole player-visible output is one 0-7 Mandate scalar gating one action/season — thin depth-to-payoff ratio.

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Thread op as Domain Action / conviction_track PT drift declared into faction turn sequence
threadwork_junctures.evidence: scale_transitions_v30 §3.5/§3.6/§5.6 (ED-673); conviction_track_v30 §1.3b (ED-676); faction_layer_v30 §7 Priority-5 Thread slot

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Thread state feeding the actual Cascade/Legitimacy/PS computation engine
threadwork_junctures.evidence: faction_behavior_v30 §3.2-§3.5 formulas take only env.* shock terms and da_outcome Keys — no Coherence/Calamity/Leap input; matches grounding-doc-03's corpus note but scoped here specifically to this engine's own math

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Deliberate thread exclusion from faction behavior
threadwork_junctures.evidence: no citation found asserting intentional exclusion — treated as gap, not design choice

legibility.note: Mission text answers 'what does each actor want' cleanly and machine-readably; 'whose position is at risk' and 'what happens if no one acts' require reconstructing across scattered Stability Triggers / Accounting steps rather than one citable line.

degenerate_candidates: Institutional Consolidation passive recovery: 'no Trigger 1-5 fired → Stability+1, Accord+1' rewards inaction, a turtle-and-wait line (faction_layer_v30 §1.3, cap in §8.2)
Single-root Cascade default for all 6 factions (faction_behavior_v30 §3.2.2) collapses the many-actor conviction-blending mechanic to one leader-stat lookup

edges.mechanism: Mandate = size-weighted saturating aggregate of settlement L/PS
conviction_track PT drift, Priority-5 Thread slot
temperament drift toward outcomes-only under Strain

directions.top-down: Omega(c) autonomous-world is contradicted at the letter by faction_layer_v30 §5.8's 'GM controls NPC faction votes.'
directions.bottom-up: The accounting substrate (named coefficients, cosine similarity) is heavy and precise but surfaces almost no direct player choice.
directions.lateral: Faction sheets are narratively thick while the L/PS mechanic they cite lives one layer down in settlement_layer — identity is prose bolted onto borrowed numbers.
directions.diagonal: Thread and Domain Echo are the designed cross-scale levers, but Thread's hook into faction_behavior's own math is only incidental.
directions.forwards: Faction systems carry ~80% of Pass 2's forward-flag load and 0/6 modules appear in the Godot skeleton — the largest unresolved cluster toward Godot.
directions.backwards: The faction stat lineup (6-stat vs 7-stat, L/PS scope) has been re-litigated at least three times in one file without yet reaching a typed engine surface.

findings.claim: faction_layer_v30 §5.8 explicitly delegates NPC faction voting to a human GM, contradicting the no-GM engine premise and Omega clause (c) autonomous world.

module_contracts.yaml marks faction_politics doc:null/home-doc-GAP despite a 1115-line CANONICAL faction_politics_v30.md (rank ladders, succession, caste) existing under a matching name in the working tree.

The 4-component Mission/Cascade/PublicExpectation/L+PS engine that determines faction disposition is entirely formula-computed with no player decision point, thinning the myriad-decisions goal beneath a narrow Domain Action menu.

The faction behavior engine's own α/β/γ/λ formulas take no Thread/Coherence/Calamity input, only generic env.* shocks — P-14 inseparability is unmet at the mechanism level here specifically, not just generally.
All 6 canonical factions default to single-root Cascade, collapsing the many-actor conviction-blending mechanic into one leader-stat lookup at scenario init.
Institutional Consolidation passive recovery grants Stability+1/Accord+1 for doing nothing a season, a candidate turtle-and-wait degenerate line.
Church's core win-path mechanic (Mass Seizure) still has 3 non-reconciled canon formulas live simultaneously, undermining legibility of the Church's central threat.
The faction stat lineup (6-stat vs 7-stat, L/PS scope) has been re-litigated three times via sequential Jordan rulings but still has no typed engine-params surface for Godot to consume.
